message:
feat(ffs-cli): mount background scrub plan + guard + daemon spawn (bd-4h62v)

Second slice of bd-4h62v (Wire ScrubDaemon into ffs mount lifecycle).
The first slice landed the MountBackgroundScrubConfig parsing /
default-resolution surface; this slice adds the runtime that
consumes that config.

New structures and functions:

MountBackgroundScrubPlan
- Captures the per-filesystem scrub layout the daemon needs:
  flavor (FsFlavor), block_size, fs_uuid, and the resolved
  Vec<GroupConfig>.
- Computed once at mount time so the daemon doesn't re-derive it
  per round.

MountBackgroundScrubGuard
- RAII handle owning the daemon's Cx (for cancellation), the
  JoinHandle<Result<ScrubDaemonMetrics>>, and the operation_id /
  scenario_id pair used as structured-log correlation.
- stop(): set Cx cancel_requested = true to wake the daemon, then
  join the thread. The Drop impl calls stop() so the daemon is
  unconditionally torn down when mount returns (clean unmount,
  panic, or process exit).
- Structured logs on stop, classified by outcome:
  - background_scrub_stopped (daemon returned Ok(metrics)) — emits
    blocks_scanned_total, blocks_corrupt_found, blocks_recovered,
    blocks_unrecoverable, scrub_rounds_completed for ops visibility.
  - background_scrub_failed (daemon returned Err) — emits the
    error reason without raising it, since failed background scrub
    must not mask the user's primary unmount path.
  - background_scrub_panicked (handle.join() returned JoinError) —
    final escape hatch so a panicking daemon doesn't propagate into
    the mount runtime.

build_mount_background_scrub_plan(image_path, open_fs)
- Branches on open_fs.ext4_superblock() vs btrfs_context() to pick
  the right group-spec builder (ext4 uses
  build_ext4_repair_group_specs, btrfs uses the analog) and
  populate flavor / block_size / fs_uuid / groups.
- Surfaces wrapped errors via context() so the user sees which
  step (e.g., "failed to compute ext4 repair group layout for
  mount background scrub") not just the underlying nested error.

open_mount_background_scrub_ledger(ledger_path)
- Opens the optional ledger file with StdOpenOptions (the alias
  imported in the prior plumbing commit) — append + create so
  multiple mount cycles accumulate evidence in one file.

run_mount_background_scrub_daemon(plan, ledger, interval, image_path,
                                  read_write, cx)
- Constructs the ScrubDaemonConfig from the resolved plan and the
  user-chosen interval, builds the underlying ScrubWithRecovery
  with .with_repair_writes_enabled(read_write) so read-only mounts
  honor the read-only-safe default landed in the prior commit, and
  drives ScrubDaemon::run_until_cancelled.

start_mount_background_scrub(...)
- The mount-side spawn point: returns Option<MountBackgroundScrubGuard>.
  If config.enabled is false, returns None and emits the
  user-visible "background scrub disabled" log line. Otherwise
  spawns the daemon thread and returns Some(guard).

Mount lifecycle integration (existing mount_cmd flow): the guard
returned here is held in mount_cmd's locals so it lives for the
duration of FUSE; when mount_cmd returns the guard's Drop runs and
the daemon is joined cleanly. No new clap flags or runtime modes
in this commit — that lands in the next slice once the per-mode
(standard / managed / per-core) hookup is decided.

bd-4h62v stays in_progress; the FUSE-mode hookup and the e2e tests
covering "mount → scrub spins → unmount" are the remaining
acceptance items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
